examples: Can you say the alphabet backwardly?
While most 2.0-compatible drives are backwardly compatible with 1.1, it is generally advisable to avoid using 1.1 unless there is no other option (due to its slow speed).
The new USB 3.0 bus-powered portable hard drive is backwardly compatible with USB 2.0 ports, ensuring that all of the user's content is completely accessible.
